sutta: once when the exalted 1 dwelt near benares in the deer pang and isi patana a number of elders the rasa who had returned from arms gathering and eaten sat together in the round hall and talked a talk on the higher dharma now from time to time the vulnerable chitta hati sariputta bro in on the talk and the wearable maha kotita said to him let not the venerable chitta hati sariputta constantly interrupt the eldest talk on the higher dhamma the variable chitta should wait until the talk is over and when he had thus spoken cheetah's friend said the verbal maha kotita should not censure the vulnerable chitta hati sariputta a wise man is a verbal chitta and able to talk to the elders on the higher dharma [Music] and wherever mahakatita replied it's a hard thing sirs for those who know not another person's ways of thought consider cerse a person who so long as he lives near the master or a fellow teacher in the holy life is the most humble of the humble the meekest of the me the quietest of the quiet and who when he leaves the master or his fellow teachers keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples men and women rajas their ministers call setters that means external ascetic leaders or their disciples living in company unhampered rude given over to gossip passion corrupts his heart and with his heart corrupted by passion he disavows the training and returns to the lower life supporters an ox a medal browser were kept tied by a rope or closed in a cow shed would he say rightly who should say never now will that meadow browser venture again to a meadow angita's friend said no indeed sir such a thing does not happen since that ox used to browsing in meadows wood on snapping its rope or breaking out of the cowshed venture down to the meadow again parable maha kotita said even sorceress where a person so long as is as he he is near the master or a fellow teacher is the most humble of the humble because of the me quietest of the quiet but who on leaving the master or his fellow teachers keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc passion corrupts his heart and he returns to the lower life consider again a person who aloof from sensual desires enters and abides in the first jana thinking i've 1 to the first jhana he keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc and returns to the lower life supposers the rain deva rains heavy rains at the 4 crossroads lays the dust and makes money would he say rightly who should say never now will dust again appear at these 4 crossroads no indeed sir since along those 4 crossroads men ox and cows might pass all the wind and heat might dry up the moisture and then the dust would appear again it is even sorceress when a person enters and abides in the first jhana and keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc he returns to the lower life then consider a person who suppressing applied and sustained thought enters and abides in the second jana thinking i have 1 to the second jannah he keeps company with monks nuns and lay disciples etc and he returns to the lower line supposes a great lake near some village or town and the rain deva were to rain great rains and cover the mussels and shells and sand and pebbles would he say rightly we should say never now in this great lake shall the muscles shells sand and pebbles appear again and cheetah's friends said no indeed sir since men ox and cows might come and drink from the great lake or wind and heat dry up the moisture and then the mussels shells sand and pebbles would appear again and in the verb said it is even sources where a person enters and abides in the second jana and keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc he returns to their lower life consider then the person who with the fading away of delight pt enters and abides in the first in the third jana thinking of 1 to the third jannah he keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc and returns to the lower line supposers last night's food please not a man filled with good food would he say rightly who should say never now shall food please this man again no indeed sir this is not the case as long as the strength from the good food remain in his body other foods shall not please that man but when that strength has gone then shall food please him again and the variable mahakata said it is even sources where a person enters and abides in the third jana and keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc he returns to the lower life consider the person who putting away pleasure and pain enters and abides in the fourth jana thinking at 1 to the fourth jana he keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc and returns to the lower life imagine sirs upon in the mountain valley windless waveless would he say rightly who should say never now on this pond shall waves appear again and they said no indeed sir sins were a squall to come very strongly from the east it would bring waves to the pond so likewise were a squall to come from the west the north or the south and then it is even sources where a person enters and abides in the fourth jana and keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc and he returns to the lower left and consider the person who paying no attention to signs enters and abides in the signless mental concentration thinking i have won to the signless mental concentration he keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples men and women rajas their ministers courses and their disciples living in company unhampered rude given over to gossip passion corrupts his heart and with his heart corrupted by passion he disavows the training and returns to the lower life supposers raja or his minister with the 4 hosts of the army were to come up to the high road and pitch their camp for 1 night in the forest and the sound of the cricket be drowned by the sound of elephant horse chariot and foot soldier by the sound of tabor drum and conch would he say rightly who should say never now in this forest shall the cricket be heard again no indeed sir this is not the case for when the raja and his and his minister have left the forest and the army have left the forest the cricket shall be heard again it is even sources where a person unattentive to science enters and abides in the signless mental concentration and thinking of 1 to that keeps company with monks nuns lay disciples etc living in company unhampered rude given to gossip passion corrupts his heart and he returns to the lower light and later the verbal chitta hati sariputta disavowed the training and returned to the lower life then chitta's friends went to the renewable mahagothita and said did the verbal mahagothita discover chittahati sariputta by mind encompassing mind concerning the thought this and that state of attainment has chita 1-2 and but he will give up the training and return to the lower life or did devas tell him this hathi sarikuta sir has won this and that but he'll return to the lower life and remember mahaquatita said robin says i discovered it by mine encompassing mine but devas also told me then the verbal cheetah's friends approached the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated they said to him lord chitta hati sarikuta has 1 to this and that state of attainment yet he has disavowed the training and returned to the lower life and the buddha said monks world before long think of renouncing the worldly life and not long after chitta hati sariputa had his hair and beard shaved off down the yellow rope and went forth from the home to the homeless life and the wearable chitta hati sariputta living alone secluded earnest adam resolved not long after entered and abort in that aim above all of the holy life realizing it here and now by his own knowledge for the sake of which clansmen rightly go forth from the home to the homeless life and he knew birth is destroyed the holy life is lived done is what is was to be done there is no more of this to come and the verb was numbered among the arahants that's the end of the suta
commentary: this sutra is quite interesting it shows that a person like the vulnerable chitta even though he has attained the jhanas and then even perhaps even this signless concentration a very still state of mind and yet he could disrobe because he mixed too much with other people because he mixed too much with other people he talked too much and then his heart was disturbed and his mind was disturbed and he uh returned to the lay life but and the vulnerable maha kotita this could read his mind that's why this album gave this warning and true enough the verbal cheetah this rope but later the buddha said he will come back he will come back as a monk and verbal cheetah did come back and after that he practiced and he attained the arahantha and this shows that a person who has 1 to jhana can steal this rope and the point here is that seclusion or aloofness is very important for a monk even if you stay in the monastery with other monks he should try not to talk too much otherwise he will lose his um supernormal states like jhana [Music] but you can see you know like a person like this cheetah because he has attained to such a high state of blessing when he disrobed and he went back to lay life and he could never never be happy because the happiness from lay life is lower than the happiness of the janik blissela so it is quite natural for him who has already attained january blister to renounce and come back to ropes again the next utah
